resources.string: Shows Safe Cleanup search with copy action.
e.g. show me shiny legendary pokemon
Describe what you want to find in plain language.
Describe what you want to find. PokeQuery turns it into a safe search string you can copy.
e.g. show me shiny legendary pokemon
Fix the errors above before copying.
Advisory only — copy stays enabled. Review matches before acting.
The assistant built this search string from your description. Review the matches in Pokémon GO before acting.
Review all matches in Pokémon GO before transferring or trading.
Could not understand that request. Try words like shiny, hundo, cleanup, trade, PvP, lucky, shadow, old, or costume.
This search string has an issue that must be fixed before copying.
This search requires review before acting.
Paste or type a search string to see what each part does.
Clipboard contains text that looks like a search string.
This screen explains the recognized tokens and exclusions in your search string.
Some tokens are not recognized. Verify them in Pokémon GO.
Includes this token in the matched results.
Excludes this token from the matched results.
During Candy transfer bonus events, extra candies can make 2x Candy Prep goals more effective. PokeQuery does not pull live event data — verify if an event is active in Pokémon GO itself.
This is a manual guide. PokeQuery doesn\'t connect to your game, so it can\'t know for sure what\'s active. Always double-check in-game!

During Community Day, candy and evolution bonuses can make Candy Prep and cleanup-style reviews more useful. This is a manual app note — PokeQuery does not pull live event data and the note may be outdated.
Manual app note. This note may be outdated. PokeQuery does not pull event data.
Manual app note. This is not live event data. PokeQuery does not pull event data.
PokeQuery uses public event notes and a safe built-in fallback. No login, tracking, or Pokémon GO account access. Always verify active bonuses in Pokémon GO.
Representative Unown check for letters and collection gaps.
Regional Pokémon can matter for trades and collection goals.
Costume catches are easy to lose during fast cleanup.
Use this as a reminder to check special backgrounds before transfer.
Raid catches often need IV, trade, and shiny review.
Use shiny checks before any storage cleanup pass.
Keep shiny and rare catches safe.
Bonuses can affect candy, trades, and storage decisions.
Review raid catches separately for IVs, shiny, and trade value.
Research encounters may contain Pokémon worth keeping.
Clear space in small batches instead of one big cleanup.
Keep trade tags and PvP/IV candidates until reviewed.
Protect rare, shiny, costumed, and background catches.
Tag rare, shiny, costume, and background catches before cleanup.
Review raid catches for IVs, shiny status, backgrounds, and trades.
Check Pokémon caught from raids and research encounters separately before transferring them.
Costumes and special backgrounds are easy to miss during cleanup.
Keep these catches until you have checked forms, backgrounds, shiny status, and trade plans.
Extra free Raid Passes, no Remote Raid Pass limit, better Premier Balls, faster Party Power.
Confirm active bonuses in Pokémon GO before making transfer decisions.
Make space, tag valuable catches, then review before transfer.
Use PokeQuery searches as a review aid only; always inspect results in Pokémon GO.

Prioritise rare or shiny-eligible legendary and mega raids. Look for highly useful attackers, exclusive or event-only appearances, and Pokémon worth lucky trading. Always review raid catches separately for IVs and shiny checks before any cleanup pass.

Road of Legends rotates five-star, Mega, and Primal raid targets. Some raid catches may have a special background. Origin Forme Dialga and Palkia may appear with Adventure Effect moves. Review raid catches separately for IVs, shiny status, backgrounds, and trade value.
Check the day’s raid list and Raid Hour. Focus on targets that still help your account, then tag valuable catches before cleanup.

Link energy (such as Fusion Energy, Solar/Lunar Energy, or Meteorites) is used for special fusions, form changes, or signature moves. Fused forms (like Dusk Mane or Dawn Wings Necrozma, or Mega Rayquaza) are some of the strongest attackers in Pokémon GO. Use PokeQuery filters to find high-IV base candidates and clear storage space before performing fusions.
Save and allocate your event energy resources carefully. Check base IVs before fusing.

Link Charges are an entry resource for Mega Raids. In-person Mega Raids can use Link Charges or a Premium Battle Pass, while the game may use a Free Raid Pass first. Remote Super Mega Raids may need both Link Charges and a Remote Raid Pass. The GO Pass: Road of Legends raises the cap to 2,000 until GO Fest 2026: Global ends.
Claim GO Pass rewards. Check the Link Charge Tool before a Mega Raid and do not waste the temporary 2,000 cap.
Road of Legends has special 2 km, 5 km, and 10 km Eggs with half hatch distance. GO Pass rewards can include incubators.
Make room in the Egg tray before spinning PokéStops. Use the free Incubator efficiently and save Super Incubators for long-distance or event Eggs.
Prepare for the upcoming event by checking off checklist tasks. Track your progress here to ensure a successful event.
Complete checklist items before event start. Always verify active bonuses manually.
Clear storage space before the event.
Tag trade candidates and keep PvP IV sweeps.
Save raid passes and incubators if relevant.
Prepare mega or primal boosts for extra candy.
Copy the relevant search from PokeQuery.
Keep shiny, background, and costume catches safe.
Before transferring, check the Today view in Pokémon GO and review the results manually.
• Full Turkish localization for Event Guide, Onboarding, Goal Detail, and Home
• Search Assistant promoted to a primary Home card; Nundo Finder moved to More Tools
• Offline manual event mode only — no live event data is fetched in this release
Event Guide uses public notes and a safe built-in fallback.
No login, tracking, ads, analytics, or Pokémon GO account access.
Turkish search tokens remain beta — verify results in Pokémon GO.
Verify Turkish localization applies to visible UI.
Check Event Guide shows online and fallback notes correctly.
Verify Search Assistant accepts Turkish input such as parlak.
Quick access to upcoming Pokémon GO events
Shows likely low-value Pokémon while excluding protected categories. Review the list yourself before transferring anything.
Finds extras for candy cleanup. Count groups by species number, so still check forms, costumes, shinies, and favorites.
Finds possible trade extras. Real trade eligibility depends on friendship, special trades, and your own plan.
Finds all perfect IV / hundo Pokémon. 4★ means 15/15/15.
Finds Pokémon without tags so you can review what still needs sorting.
Candidate search only. Final PvP rank depends on species, level, and IV spread.
Finds older or distance-relevant Pokémon to review for trades. Lucky chance is never guaranteed, so check every result.
Builds a custom search string. PokeQuery can warn about syntax, but you must review the matches before acting.
This is an inspection query. It shows matches but does not suggest a destructive action.
Inspection queries are safe to run: they only display Pokémon. Still review the matches so you understand what is in your storage.
Open the search and read the matches.
No transfer or trade is suggested.
Use it to learn what you own.
A narrow query. Still review the matches before acting.
Low-risk strings are deliberately narrow, but a valuable Pokémon can still appear. Skim the results before transferring or trading.
Keep anything you are unsure about.
Tag valuable Pokémon to protect them.
This query is action-adjacent. Review carefully before transferring or trading.
Medium-risk strings are designed for cleanup or trading decisions. Valuable Pokémon may appear in the matches. Do not act on them blindly.
Never transfer shiny, legendary, or favorite Pokémon by accident.
When in doubt, keep the Pokémon.
A broad or high-value query. Inspect every match before acting.
High-risk strings can surface very valuable Pokémon. Treat every match as worth checking before any transfer or trade.
Protect valuable Pokémon with tags or favorites.
This finds Pokémon you might want to transfer. But it can also catch valuable ones by mistake.

This search looks for Pokémon with low stats that are usually safe to transfer. But sometimes a shiny, a hundo (perfect IVs), or a Pokémon you tagged as special can slip through. Always look at each result before you hit transfer.
Look at every single Pokémon in the results.
Keep anything shiny, perfect (hundo), or you marked as favorite.
This finds extra Pokémon to transfer for candy. But it counts by species, not by form or costume.

This search helps you find duplicates to transfer during 2x candy events. The count is based on the Pokédex number, so a shiny Pikachu and a costume Pikachu count as the same "slot". You might accidentally transfer a rare form. Check each one.
Check every match — shinies and costumes count as the same species.
Keep anything with a special tag or favorite star.
This finds untraded duplicates for trading. But friendship level and trade limits aren\'t in the search.

This search finds Pokémon you haven\'t traded yet. But being able to trade depends on your friendship level with the other trainer. Also, special trades (shiny, legendary, new Pokédex) are limited to once per day. The search can\'t know these limits.
This only shows your perfect 15/15/15 IV Pokémon. No transfers suggested.
This search only finds Pokémon with perfect IVs (4★ / hundo). It\'s an inspection tool — it doesn\'t suggest transferring anything. Use it to admire your best Pokémon or pick which to power up.
Open the search and enjoy your hundos.
No transfer or trade is suggested.
Use it to learn what you own.
This only shows your 0/0/0 IV Pokémon. No transfers suggested.
This search only finds Pokémon with exactly 0 attack, 0 defense, 0 HP IVs (nundos). It\'s for collecting or curiosity. It doesn\'t suggest transferring anything.
Open the search and see your nundos.
No transfer or trade is suggested.
Use it to learn what you own.
This finds PvP candidates. Final rank depends on species, level, and IV spread.

This search finds Pokémon that *might* be good for Great League or Ultra League. But the actual PvP rank depends on the exact species, its level, and how its IVs are spread. This is a starting point — you still need to check each one in a PvP simulator.
Check each candidate in a PvP simulator (like PvPoke).
No transfer or trade is suggested.
Use it to find candidates to test.
This finds older or far-away Pokémon for lucky trades. But trade eligibility and lucky rate aren\'t guaranteed.

This search finds Pokémon that *might* become lucky when traded (older than 2 years or caught 100km+ away). But both trainers must be able to trade, and the lucky rate is never 100%. Also, already-traded Pokémon can\'t become lucky again. Valuable Pokémon can appear in results.
Check friendship level and trade eligibility first.
Already-traded Pokémon can\'t become lucky again.
Why this search needs a quick check
This finds Pokémon without tags. Most are safe to review, but check for shinies or hundos.

This search shows Pokémon that don\'t have any tags. Usually these are safe to consider for transfer. But a shiny or perfect IV Pokémon without a tag can still appear. Give the results a quick scroll.
Keep anything shiny, perfect (hundo), or you\'re unsure about.
Tag valuable Pokémon to protect them next time.
You built this search. It does exactly what you typed — including risky things.
Custom searches run exactly what you write. If you include broad terms or forget exclusions, valuable Pokémon can appear. PokeQuery can\'t protect you from your own query. Review every match.
Read your own search string carefully.
Check every single match before acting.
The assistant built this from your words. Always review the matches in Pokémon GO.

The assistant converts your plain language into a search string. It might not capture everything you meant, or it might include things you didn\'t expect. The search is a suggestion — you decide what to do with the results.
Review every match in Pokémon GO.
This is a review aid only. Always inspect matches in Pokémon GO before transferring or trading.
The \'|\' operator is unsupported and was replaced with \',\'.
Count output: Count is based on Pokédex species number and may not distinguish shiny/form/costume differences.
Trade disclaimer: Real trade eligibility depends on friendship level and cannot be guaranteed by search strings.
Review the search and its warnings before copying. PokeQuery only creates text — always inspect matches in Pokémon GO before acting.
Useful for collectors who want to keep 0★ Pokémon.
Protects against accidental transfer of shinies.
Candidate search only. PokeQuery does not calculate PvP rank; review each match manually.
Already-traded Pokémon cannot become Lucky via trade again.
This is a review aid, not a command.
Review all matches in Pokémon GO.
Safer search strings for Pokémon GO
What do you want to find?
Pick a goal and we\'ll build a safe search string for it.
PokeQuery is offline first. It never connects to Pokémon GO, Niantic, or asks for credentials. Your account is completely safe.
Generated strings are search filters, not automation. Always manually review the highlighted Pokémon in-game before transferring or trading.
Event Guide provides curated notes and search tips for active bonuses. PokeQuery does not track you or require a network connection.
Find what is safe to review before you transfer, trade, or clean up.
Build the right search in seconds
Use safe defaults for cleanup, candy, trades, PvP, Hundos, and Nundos.
Cleanup, candy, trades, PvP, hundos, nundos, and event prep.
Warnings remind you to check shiny, costume, favorite, and tagged Pokémon.
PokeQuery never touches your account. You paste the search yourself.
PokeQuery creates search text. You stay in control: copy it, open Pokémon GO yourself, and review before acting.
Uses public event notes with a safe built-in fallback. No login, tracking, or Pokémon GO account access.
Controls the text of this app only. Pokémon GO itself is not affected.
Controls the language of the text you paste into Pokémon GO.
Offer to explain a search string found on your clipboard.
PokeQuery only creates text. You copy and paste it yourself.
No login, no tracking, no ads, no analytics, no Pokémon GO account access.
PokeQuery is an independent helper app.
It only generates search strings. It never connects to Pokémon GO.
No login, no tracking, no ads, no analytics.
Every generated string is a review aid. Always inspect matches in Pokémon GO before transferring or trading.
App Language applies to this app only and is a foundation for future translation. Pokémon GO itself is not affected.
Auto uses your device language when verified; otherwise it falls back safely. Localized search terms are partial, so always review results in Pokémon GO before acting.
Some search terms are unverified and will fall back to English. Always review results in Pokémon GO before acting.
PokeQuery is an independent helper app and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Niantic, The Pokémon Company, or Nintendo.
No email app found. Please email caglar@caglardinc.com manually.
Send feedback by email. No data leaves your device unless you send the email.
Ready-made search strings for common goals. Tap to preview, then copy.
Always review matches in Pokémon GO before transferring.
Find Pokémon caught in the last 7 days
Find species you have more than 2 of (excludes protected categories)
Find Pokémon that can be evolved right now
Find Pokémon caught in the last 3 days
Find untraded Pokémon over a year old
Find untraded Pokémon caught over 100 km away
Find shiny, legendary, or mythical Pokémon
Find low attack / high bulk candidates under 1500 CP
Find low attack / high bulk candidates under 2500 CP
Special trades are limited to 1 per day.
Event data loaded from cache (last updated: %1$s).
Event data is invalid. Using local fallback.
Event data is up to date (last updated: %1$s).
Your saved presets will appear here.
Your recent queries will appear here.
Keep track of your queries here.
No terms found for your search.
Try out queries without affecting real Pokemon.
This removes all saved search strings. This cannot be undone.
This removes all recently copied search strings. This cannot be undone.
This restores language settings to defaults. Favorites and history are kept. This cannot be undone.
Spot-check a few results before mass transferring to be extra safe.
The count is based on species number. It may not distinguish between shiny, form, costume, gender, or size variants.
Try natural language like \'shiny legendary pokemon\' or \'hundo for ultra league\'. The assistant will translate it into a game-ready search string.
What should I do in PokeQuery?
Pull to refresh, or check back later. We only show notes from our public feed — no account access.
Choose event — updates the guide below
